name: Architext 1.7.9 Convergent Instruction-Rules Repairs
summary: Fixes the doctor phantom-repair loop field-reported by a consuming repository on 1.7.8: the instruction-rules category re-advertised and re-applied byte-identical AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md rewrites on every run, forever. The migration plan now carries a convergence gate — a rewrite is planned only when it would actually change the written bytes (via a normalization helper shared with the writer, so gate and writer cannot drift) or when the file contributes a not-yet-migrated rule — converging doctor, sync, and the serve repair endpoint at one chokepoint. The pointer-section splice is idempotent (it previously ate the separator newline its own append branch inserts, so a freshly migrated file triggered one extra rewrite; both historical spacings are now fixed points, so no existing consumer file churns on upgrade). Repair attribution names the file actually written on both the advertised (doctor --json/status) and applied sides — rewrites no longer report as rules.json, the diagnostic blind spot that obscured the field investigation. First fix produced end to end under the code-rca skill: reproduced RED at CLI and function level, falsifiable fixed-point test, three distinct defects enumerated instead of one.
updateSource: Shipped 2026-07-17: GitHub release v1.7.9 published 2026-07-17T10:23:03Z with all five native binaries + SHA256SUMS attached by publish.yml. Install path verified end to end: darwin-arm64 asset downloaded, SHA256 matched the manifest, binary reports 1.7.9. Ceremony held at the open release PR per maintainer directive (code-rca skill handover), then completed on his go. All scope merged via swarm-reviewed PR #268 (cp-11+cp-12); core 162/162, cli 38/38, serve green, clippy clean; 3-run CLI convergence byte-verified.

required.title: Instruction-rules repairs converge: no phantom re-advertise/re-apply
required.summary: planned_instruction_rule_migration pushed rewriteFiles/ambiguousFiles unconditionally, so any already-migrated instruction file re-triggered the identical rewrite on every doctor/sync run (real but byte-identical writes reported as applied — field-hit on a consuming repo). The plan now gates each file on (rewrite would change the written bytes, using ensure_trailing_newline shared with the writer) OR (the file contributed a new candidate rule after dedup). Fixed-point regression tests pin convergence; a repair-twice-on-tempdir integration test pins the gate/writer normalization invariant end to end.

required.title: Pointer-section splice is idempotent across both historical spacings
required.summary: upsert_rule_pointer was not a fixed point of its own output: the managed-section splice consumed the separator newline the append branch inserts, so a freshly migrated file planned one more rewrite, and the separator re-supply initially keyed on heading position rather than the splice's own preceding-byte condition (review-caught: a heading substring matched mid-line would have gained a newline never present in the source). The splice now mirrors exactly its own consumption condition; both blank-line and no-blank-line historical spacings are fixed points, so no consumer file churns on upgrade. Module doc records the intentional divergence from the historical JS port (which had the same non-idempotence) so it is not 'fixed back'.

required.title: Repairs attribute to the file actually written, advertised and applied
required.summary: Instruction-file rewrites were attributed to rules.json — a hardcoded per-category display path — on both the advertised list (doctor --json/status) and the applied list, hiding the true write target during the field investigation (a diagnostic blind spot repaired as part of the RCA per the code-rca contract). RepairOutcome carries the written path through apply, and the status side derives the path from the change itself; rewrites now report AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.

workstreams.name: Doctor trustworthiness
workstreams.summary: Continues the 1.7.8 arc: every repair category converges (advertise → apply → silence) and reports honestly which file it touched.

evidence.label: PR #268 merged to develop (cp-11 + cp-12, swarm-reviewed, verify-rust green)
Convergence regression suite: fixed-point, mid-line heading, heading-at-start, pointer-missing guard, repair-twice-on-tempdir; 3-run CLI convergence byte-verified (cargo test -p architext-core --lib)
